Best Software · Masonry Contractors

Best Software for Masonry Contractors (2026)

Estimating brick, block, and stone work requires precision. These platforms handle the math — and the business.

Masonry contractors deal with complex material calculations — unit counts, mortar coverage, waste factors for cuts — combined with labor-intensive work where productivity rates matter enormously. The best software for masonry helps you estimate accurately, track actual costs, and manage crews across multiple sites.

Quick picks

SoftwareBest forStarting price
BuildertrendMasonry subs on residential/light commercial projects$499/mo
JobTreadJob costing and margin tracking$99/mo
Contractor ForemanAffordable all-in-one for small masonry crews$49/mo
ProcoreLarge commercial masonry subcontractorsCustom
Houzz ProResidential masonry and hardscape specialists$85/mo

1. Buildertrend — Best for masonry subs working with GCs

Masonry subcontractors on residential and light commercial projects benefit most from being on the same platform as their GC customers. Buildertrend dominates the residential construction space — if your GCs use it, you should know how to operate within it.

  • Receive and confirm task assignments from GCs
  • Daily log submissions with in-progress photos
  • Change order requests for extra scope (added courses, design changes)
  • Material purchase orders tied to specific jobs
  • Subcontractor payment applications through the system

Masonry subs: Buildertrend fluency makes you easier to hire. That's a real business advantage.

Explore Buildertrend

2. JobTread — Best for masonry job costing

Masonry is one of the most labor-intensive trades, and productivity rates vary significantly based on bond pattern, block size, and site conditions. JobTread's real-time job costing helps you understand actual cost per unit, so you can sharpen future bids and catch overruns before they compound.

  • Estimate templates with material and labor line items
  • Real-time budget vs. actual as materials arrive and labor is logged
  • Purchase orders for block, brick, mortar, rebar — with committed cost tracking
  • Change order workflow with margin control
  • $99/month — accessible for masonry operations of most sizes

If you bid masonry work by the unit and need to know your actual cost — JobTread delivers that visibility.

Try JobTread

3. Contractor Foreman — Best budget platform

Small masonry operations — a few crews, under $2M revenue — will find Contractor Foreman covers the essentials without the enterprise price tag. Estimates, time tracking, invoices, and project notes all in one platform at $49/month.

4. Procore — For commercial masonry subcontractors

Large commercial masonry subcontractors often work within Procore because their GC requires it for submittals, RFIs, and safety documentation. Procore's commercial-grade features are excellent for that environment but expensive and complex for smaller operations.

5. Houzz Pro — For decorative and residential masonry

Masonry contractors who specialize in decorative work — custom fireplaces, stone facades, hardscaping, garden walls — will find Houzz Pro's homeowner-facing marketplace valuable. Homeowners searching Houzz for masonry and stonework represent a high-intent lead pool.

Decorative masonry and hardscape contractors: Houzz Pro puts you in front of homeowners actively planning these projects.

Try Houzz Pro

Key features for masonry software

  • Unit-based estimating: Price by the brick/block/square foot with waste factors
  • Material procurement: Manage deliveries — the wrong block arriving on day 3 of a 5-day job is a disaster
  • Crew time tracking: Masonry is labor-intensive; accurate time tracking is essential to knowing your real costs
  • Photo documentation: Before/during/after photos protect you from disputes about scope and quality
  • Change order management: Added courses, design changes, and extra work need documented approval before you do it
